description: Harness - An Opinionated Agent Orchestration System that moves all deterministic operations out of LLM prompts into a Python orchestration layer

userStories.title: HarnessConfig Pydantic model and harness init command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a validated configuration model and scaffold command so that I can initialize a harness project with sensible defaults.
acceptanceCriteria: HarnessConfig Pydantic v2 model in config.py with fields: model, runtime, max_iterations, prd_path, progress_path, tool_allowlist, tool_denylist, language_profiles, max_parallel_agents, max_retries, max_prompt_tokens, verification_command
harness init command scaffolds harness_config.py from a template with all config fields and inline comments
harness init is idempotent — prompts before overwriting an existing config
Config loads and validates successfully from a harness_config.py file via importlib
Invalid config fields raise a clear Pydantic ValidationError with field name and reason
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Core data models (PRD, Story, Progress, Diagnostic)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want typed Pydantic models for all internal data structures so that every layer of the harness has validated, IDE-autocomplete-friendly types.
acceptanceCriteria: Story model in core/models/prd.py: id, title, description, acceptanceCriteria, priority, passes, notes, dependsOn (list[str], default []), parallelGroup (str | None)
PRD model: project, branchName, description, userStories (list[Story])
ProgressEntry model in core/models/progress.py: story_id, timestamp (ISO-8601), status (pending | in_progress | passed | failed), files_changed (list[str]), body (markdown str)
Diagnostic model in core/models/verify.py: file, line, column, severity, message, rule
AgentResult model in core/models/output.py: status (complete | failed | partial), completion_signal_found (bool), files_modified (list[str]), raw_output (str)
All models serialise to/from JSON without loss
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: ExecutionEnvironment protocol and LocalWorktreeEnvironment
userStories.description: As a harness architect, I want all agent execution to go through an ExecutionEnvironment interface so that swapping from local to container execution requires only a new implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: ExecutionEnvironment Protocol in execution/base.py with async methods: setup(story) -> Context, execute(command, *args) -> tuple[int, str, str], get_root() -> Path, async teardown()
Context dataclass holds: root_path, worktree_path, branch_name, story_id
Protocol has network_policy field typed as Literal['full', 'restricted', 'gapped'] with default 'full'
LocalWorktreeEnvironment in execution/local.py implements the full protocol using local filesystem and subprocess
ContainerEnvironment in execution/container.py is stubbed — all methods raise NotImplementedError with a descriptive message
All harness managers (Claim, Worktree, Branch) accept an ExecutionEnvironment instance rather than calling os/subprocess directly
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: ClaimManager — atomic story claiming
userStories.description: As a harness system, I want atomic claim creation and release so that two parallel harness processes can never claim the same story.
acceptanceCriteria: ClaimManager in core/claim.py with methods: claim(story_id) -> bool, release(story_id) -> None, is_claimed(story_id) -> bool, cleanup_stale(max_age_minutes: int = 30) -> list[str]
claim() uses os.open with O_CREAT | O_EXCL flags to atomically create the claim file — returns False without raising if already claimed
Claim files written to {root_path}/tasks/claims/{story_id}.claim containing ISO-8601 timestamp and PID
cleanup_stale() removes claim files older than max_age_minutes and returns a list of cleaned story IDs
release() deletes the claim file; safe to call when no claim exists
ClaimManager accepts root_path: Path as constructor argument — no global state
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: WorktreeManager — git worktree lifecycle
userStories.description: As a harness system, I want programmatic git worktree creation and teardown so that each story runs in an isolated working directory.
acceptanceCriteria: WorktreeManager in core/worktree.py with methods: create(story_id, branch_name) -> Path, teardown(story_id) -> None, get_path(story_id) -> Path | None
create() adds a git worktree at {root_path}/.harness/worktrees/{story_id}/, creates and checks out a new branch named branch_name
create() symlinks .env from the main repo root into the worktree if .env exists
create() runs the project's dep-install command (configurable, e.g. uv sync) inside the worktree after creation
teardown() removes the worktree via git worktree remove --force and cleans up the directory
WorktreeManager accepts root_path: Path and env: ExecutionEnvironment as constructor arguments
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: BranchManager and ArchiveManager
userStories.description: As a harness system, I want git branch management and PRD archiving so that completed features are merged cleanly and old PRDs are preserved.
acceptanceCriteria: BranchManager in core/branch.py with methods: create(branch_name, base_branch) -> None, merge(branch_name, base_branch) -> bool, has_conflicts(branch_name) -> bool
merge() returns True on success; returns False (does not raise) when merge conflicts are detected — harness logs and pauses for human
ArchiveManager in core/archive.py with methods: archive(prd_path, progress_path, archive_dir) -> Path
archive() creates {archive_dir}/YYYY-MM-DD-{prd-name}/ and copies prd.json + progress file into it
Both managers accept root_path: Path and operate through ExecutionEnvironment for git commands
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: ProgressManager and PRD-scoped progress files
userStories.description: As a developer, I want progress tracking scoped per-PRD so that each feature's history is self-contained and I can query context-window-friendly summaries.
acceptanceCriteria: ProgressManager in core/progress.py with methods: append(entry: ProgressEntry) -> None, summarize(max_tokens: int) -> str, get_entries(story_id: str | None) -> list[ProgressEntry], select_context(current_story_id, changed_files) -> str
Each PRD gets a companion file at {root_path}/progress/prd-{name}.progress.md
Global codebase patterns file at {root_path}/progress/codebase-patterns.md
Progress entries written with machine-readable YAML frontmatter (story_id, timestamp, status, files_changed) followed by markdown body
summarize() fits the most recent entries and global patterns within max_tokens using tiktoken counting
select_context() returns: global patterns + current PRD entries + entries from other PRDs that overlap on changed_files
ProgressManager accepts root_path: Path constructor argument
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: DagScheduler — dependency graph and parallel execution planning
userStories.description: As a developer, I want PRD stories scheduled via a dependency graph so that independent stories run in parallel and the harness can compute execution waves.
acceptanceCriteria: DagScheduler in core/scheduler.py with methods: build(stories: list[Story]) -> None, get_ready() -> list[Story], mark_complete(story_id: str) -> None, mark_failed(story_id: str) -> None, get_critical_path() -> list[str], get_waves() -> list[list[str]], has_cycle() -> bool
build() raises ValueError with cycle details if a dependency cycle is detected — uses graphlib.TopologicalSorter
get_ready() returns stories whose dependsOn list is fully satisfied (all deps marked complete) and that are not yet claimed or complete
Stories with empty dependsOn are immediately ready
get_waves() returns sequential groups where all stories in a group can execute in parallel
harness plan command prints: wave list with story IDs and titles, critical path, total wave count
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: LanguageProfile Protocol and auto-detection
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a pluggable language profile protocol so that any language's native toolchain can be integrated uniformly.
acceptanceCriteria: LanguageProfile Protocol in profiles/base.py with methods: lint(file: Path) -> list[Diagnostic], format(file: Path) -> str, typecheck(project: Path) -> list[Diagnostic], get_symbols(file: Path) -> list[Symbol], find_references(symbol_name: str, project: Path) -> list[Location]
Symbol dataclass: name, kind (function | class | variable | import | other), line, column, file
Location dataclass: file, line, column, context_line (the source line as a string)
detect_profile(root: Path) -> str | None function that returns language key by checking: pyproject.toml → 'python', tsconfig.json → 'typescript', go.mod → 'go', Cargo.toml → 'rust'
load_profile(language: str) -> LanguageProfile function that imports harness/profiles/{language}.py and returns the profile instance
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Python language profile (mypy, ruff, pyright, tree-sitter)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Python language profile so that the harness has structural code understanding for Python projects.
acceptanceCriteria: PythonProfile in profiles/python.py implements LanguageProfile Protocol
lint() shells out to ruff check --output-format=json and parses the JSON array of diagnostics
format() shells out to ruff format --diff and returns the formatted content
typecheck() shells out to mypy --output json and parses the per-line JSON output
get_symbols() uses tree-sitter (tree-sitter-python grammar) to extract function, class, and top-level variable definitions
find_references() shells out to pyright --outputjson and extracts reference locations
All subprocess calls use asyncio.create_subprocess_exec and are awaited
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: TypeScript language profile (tsc, eslint, prettier, tsserver)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a TypeScript language profile so that the harness has structural code understanding for TypeScript projects.
acceptanceCriteria: TypeScriptProfile in profiles/typescript.py implements LanguageProfile Protocol
lint() shells out to eslint --format=json and parses the JSON diagnostics array
format() shells out to prettier --write and returns the formatted file content
typecheck() shells out to tsc --noEmit --pretty false and parses the compiler output
get_symbols() uses tree-sitter (tree-sitter-typescript grammar) to extract function, class, interface, and type alias definitions
find_references() communicates with tsserver via its stdio JSON protocol to resolve cross-file references
All subprocess calls use asyncio.create_subprocess_exec and are awaited
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: MemoryIndex — vector DB with semantic search
userStories.description: As a harness system, I want a vector database that indexes progress entries, patterns, and code summaries so agents can retrieve relevant context via similarity search instead of receiving full file dumps.
acceptanceCriteria: MemoryIndex in memory/index.py with methods: index_progress(entry: ProgressEntry) -> None, index_pattern(content: str, tags: list[str]) -> None, index_code_summary(file: Path, symbols: list[Symbol]) -> None, search(query: str, top_k: int = 5) -> list[MemoryResult]
MemoryResult dataclass in memory/models.py: content, source_type (progress | pattern | code), score (float), metadata (dict)
Storage uses SQLite with sqlite-vss extension at {root_path}/.harness/memory.db
Embeddings generated via fastembed (ONNX-based) as default provider; optional OpenAI embeddings API configurable via HarnessConfig
Optional openai embeddings: if HarnessConfig.embedding_provider == 'openai', use openai.Embedding.create instead of fastembed
.harness/memory.db is added to .gitignore on first creation
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: PromptBuilder with token ceiling and Jinja2 templates
userStories.description: As a harness system, I want to assemble a minimal, focused system prompt for each agent invocation that stays under a token ceiling.
acceptanceCriteria: PromptBuilder in prompt/builder.py with method: build(story: Story, context: PromptContext) -> str
PromptContext dataclass: patterns (str), prd_progress (str), available_tools (list[str]), language_guidance (str), base_branch (str), worktree_dir (str)
Prompt assembled from Jinja2 templates in prompt/templates/: role.j2, story.j2, patterns.j2, tools.j2, language.j2
Template variables {{STORY_ID}}, {{BASE_BRANCH}}, {{WORKTREE_DIR}} resolved by the harness — never left as placeholders in the output
Assembled prompt includes 'MUST USE' directive for AST/lint/typecheck tools when profile is active
Token count computed via tiktoken; when assembled prompt exceeds max_prompt_tokens config value, the lowest-priority section (language guidance, then patterns) is truncated with a warning log
Typical assembled prompt is under 2000 tokens for a standard story
Prompt includes zero lifecycle instructions (no worktree setup, branch creation, or claiming instructions)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: MCP server — stdio transport and lifecycle management
userStories.description: As an agent, I want a local MCP server managed by the harness so that I can query codebase context on demand without relying on prompt stuffing.
acceptanceCriteria: MCP server in mcp/server.py using the mcp Python SDK with stdio transport
Harness starts the MCP server as a subprocess via asyncio.create_subprocess_exec; server is torn down when the story completes or harness exits
MCP server config (server command, args, env) is auto-generated and written to the Copilot MCP settings file at ~/.config/copilot/mcp.json (or platform equivalent)
server.py exposes a start() coroutine and a stop() coroutine; stop() sends SIGTERM and waits for graceful exit
Server registers all tools from mcp/tools.py on startup
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: MCP tool implementations (PRD status, patterns, progress, code intelligence)
userStories.description: As an agent, I want read-only MCP tools that expose PRD status, patterns, progress, symbols, diagnostics, references, and semantic search so that I can pull context without prompt stuffing.
acceptanceCriteria: get_prd_status tool returns: list of stories with id, title, passes, priority — filtered to current PRD from config
get_patterns tool uses MemoryIndex.search(query, top_k=5) to return semantically relevant patterns rather than dumping the full patterns file
get_progress tool returns the current PRD's progress entries, most recent first, up to a configurable limit
get_file_symbols tool calls active LanguageProfile.get_symbols(file) and returns a summary (name, kind, line) — not the full AST
get_diagnostics tool calls active LanguageProfile.lint(file) and optionally LanguageProfile.typecheck(project) and returns Diagnostic list
search_references tool calls active LanguageProfile.find_references(symbol_name, project) and returns Location list
search_memory tool delegates to MemoryIndex.search(query, top_k) and returns MemoryResult list
All 7 tools are read-only — no writes to harness state
Each tool returns a structured JSON-serialisable response
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: AgentOutputParser — real-time stream parsing and completion detection
userStories.description: As a harness system, I want to parse agent stdout in real-time so I can detect completion, failure, and partial-progress signals and decide whether to commit, retry, or escalate.
acceptanceCriteria: AgentOutputParser in core/output.py with async method: parse_stream(stdout: asyncio.StreamReader, stderr: asyncio.StreamReader) -> AgentResult
Detects completion signal: configurable string, default '<promise>COMPLETE</promise>'
Detects failure signals: configurable list of strings the agent may emit to indicate it cannot complete
On completion: checks git status in the worktree — sets AgentResult.files_modified from the uncommitted/committed diff
On partial completion (agent ran out of context): sets status='partial' and logs state for narrowed retry
All completion, failure, and partial events logged with structured metadata (story_id, timestamp, signal_text, files_modified)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: VerificationGate — post-completion lint/typecheck and retry logic
userStories.description: As a harness system, I want automated verification checks between agent completion and merge so that broken code never reaches the base branch.
acceptanceCriteria: VerificationGate in core/verify.py with async method: run(story: Story, worktree: Path, profile: LanguageProfile, retries_remaining: int) -> tuple[bool, list[Diagnostic]]
Default checks: profile.lint(worktree), profile.typecheck(worktree); optional project-level test command from HarnessConfig.verification_command
On success (zero errors): returns (True, [])
On failure with retries_remaining > 0: returns (False, diagnostics) — caller re-invokes agent with diagnostics appended as 'Fix these issues'
On failure with retries_remaining == 0: preserves worktree, marks story as failed in PRD JSON, logs full diagnostic output, prints human-readable alert to stderr
Gate result written as a ProgressEntry with structured metadata
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Workflow engine — DAG of agent stages with state persistence
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to define multi-agent workflows as DAGs of named stages so that complex operations are broken into specialised passes that can resume after a crash.
acceptanceCriteria: WorkflowStage dataclass in workflows/engine.py: name, prompt_override (str | None), tool_permissions (list[str] | None), max_iterations (int), success_criteria (str | None)
Workflow dataclass: name, stages (list[WorkflowStage]), description
WorkflowEngine.run(workflow, story, context) executes stages sequentially, passing prior stage output as context to the next
Workflow state persisted as JSON at {root_path}/.harness/workflow-state-{story_id}.json after each stage completes
WorkflowEngine.resume(story_id) reads state file and skips already-completed stages, continuing from last successful stage
Parallel stages supported: WorkflowStage.parallel_group (str | None) — stages with the same group name execute via asyncio.gather
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Built-in workflows — implement, review, refactor, full-cycle
userStories.description: As a developer, I want built-in workflow definitions for common operations so that I can run harness with a named workflow without writing configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: Four built-in Workflow instances in workflows/builtins.py: IMPLEMENT, REVIEW, REFACTOR, FULL_CYCLE
IMPLEMENT: single stage — standard implement prompt, all tools allowed, max_iterations from config
REVIEW: single stage — repo-review prompt override, read-only tool set, max_iterations=1
REFACTOR: single stage — refactor prompt override with AST MUST USE directive, all tools, max_iterations from config
FULL_CYCLE: four sequential stages — implement → lint-fix → review → address-review; each stage has appropriate prompt override and tool permissions
get_workflow(name: str) -> Workflow function raises ValueError with available names if name not found
harness run --workflow <name> executes the named workflow via WorkflowEngine
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: harness run — full orchestration loop with parallel execution
userStories.description: As a developer, I want harness run to orchestrate the full agent lifecycle end-to-end so that I can invoke it once and have stories claimed, executed, verified, and merged automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: harness run reads HarnessConfig, loads PRD JSON, builds DagScheduler graph
Calls DagScheduler.get_ready() and dispatches up to max_parallel_agents stories concurrently via asyncio.gather

Per-story loop: ClaimManager.claim → WorktreeManager.create → detect/load LanguageProfile → MemoryIndex.search → MCP server start → PromptBuilder.build → agent invocation → AgentOutputParser.parse_stream → VerificationGate.run → ProgressManager.append → MemoryIndex.index_progress → BranchManager.merge → WorktreeManager.teardown → ClaimManager.release → PRD update
On agent failure with retries remaining: re-invokes with diagnostics appended; retries count against HarnessConfig.max_retries
On unrecoverable failure: preserves worktree, marks story failed, releases claim, continues to next ready story
After each story completes: calls DagScheduler.mark_complete/mark_failed and re-evaluates ready stories
Exits cleanly when DAG is exhausted or max_iterations reached
harness run --dry-run prints what it would do without invoking the agent
harness run --workflow <name> uses WorkflowEngine instead of the default single-stage loop
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: harness plan and harness status CLI commands
userStories.description: As a developer, I want plan and status commands so that I can inspect the execution plan and current story states before and during a run.
acceptanceCriteria: harness plan reads HarnessConfig and PRD JSON, builds DagScheduler, prints: wave number, story IDs and titles per wave, critical path story IDs in order, total wave count
harness status reads current claims directory and PRD JSON, prints per-story status: pending | claimed (with PID and age) | passed | failed
Both commands exit with code 0 on success; code 1 on config / PRD parse errors with a clear error message to stderr
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: MCP catalog — recommended server configs and harness init integration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the harness to ship with recommended MCP server configurations and briefings so that agents can leverage external tools when available.
acceptanceCriteria: mcp_catalog/ directory at package root with four JSON configs: github.json, filesystem.json, browser.json, database.json
Each config file contains: server_package, install_command, required_env_vars (list), capability_description, agent_usage_guide (when to use this tool, 2-3 sentences)
harness init prompts the user to select from the catalog and writes chosen server configs to .harness/mcp-servers.json
PromptBuilder includes an 'Available External Tools' section listing enabled MCP servers with one-line usage notes when .harness/mcp-servers.json exists
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
